question: Which one is true for password?
options: Can contain letters, numbers and symbols
subject: Computer Security
explanation: Passwords are case sensitive, can have variable length, and contain letters, numbers and symbols.

question: Which is not an operator?
subject: Programming
explanation: Semicolon is a statement terminator, not an operator.

question: The orientation of the page in which the printed lines run the width of the page is called..........
subject: Word Processing
explanation: Landscape orientation has lines running across the width.

question: The labels defining in a worksheet structure
options: Are comprised of numbers and the equals sign
subject: Spreadsheet
explanation: Labels in worksheet begin with an equals sign (=).

question: To move to the last column in a worksheet, press..........
subject: Spreadsheet
explanation: Ctrl+End moves to the last used cell.

question: What will be happen when you click the border between two columns?
options: Widen the column width as contents
Widen the row height as contents
subject: Spreadsheet
explanation: Double-clicking column border auto-fits column width.

question: A worksheet contains approximately how many rows?
subject: Spreadsheet
explanation: Excel worksheet contains 65536 rows.

question: To select multiple, non-adjacent worksheets in a workbook.
options: Press Ctrl and click another tab
Press Alt and click another tab
Press Shift and click another tab
Drag the mouse pointer through the tabs
subject: Spreadsheet
explanation: Ctrl+Click selects non-adjacent worksheet tabs.

question: By default, how is text horizontal aligned inside a cell in Excel?
subject: Spreadsheet
explanation: Text is left-aligned by default in Excel.

question: Which option restrict the values that can be entered into a cell?
subject: Spreadsheet
explanation: Data Validation restricts cell input values.

question: To display the heading as slanted text to save space..........
options: Select slanted Text on the font tab
Select Text Orientation on the Alignment tab
Center Across selection on the Alignment tab
subject: Spreadsheet
explanation: Text Orientation on Alignment tab allows slanted headings.

question: What will be the correct answer, if user enters the 3 P into a cell?
subject: Spreadsheet
explanation: Excel interprets '3 P' as 3:00 PM time format.

question: The formula uses its own reference is called..........
subject: Spreadsheet
explanation: A formula referring to its own cell creates a circular reference.

question: What is the function of text to column?
options: Allows you to separate delimited text across the columns automatically
Converts the selected text to table
It automatically resizes the cell size whenever a large text is added in the cell
subject: Spreadsheet
explanation: Text to Columns splits delimited text into separate columns.

question: The maximum number of characters can be stored in a cell is..........
subject: Spreadsheet
explanation: Excel cell can store up to 32000 characters.

question: Goal Seek appears at the.......... Menu
subject: Spreadsheet
explanation: Goal Seek is found in the Tools menu.

question: Which shortcut key is used to hide column?
subject: Spreadsheet
explanation: Ctrl+9 hides the selected column in Excel.

question: The maximum length of formula is..........
subject: Spreadsheet
explanation: Maximum formula length in Excel is 1024 characters.

question: The shortcut key define name is..........
subject: Spreadsheet
explanation: Ctrl+F3 opens the Define Name dialog.

question: The maximum number of sheet within any workbook is..........
subject: Spreadsheet
explanation: Modern Excel allows unlimited sheets (previously 255).

question: The format dddd in date will display..........
subject: Spreadsheet
explanation: dddd format displays full day name like Sunday.

question: The key press moves the cell pointer on pane of another pane in split window.
subject: Spreadsheet
explanation: F6 moves between panes in a split window.

question: The referencing styles used in Excel..........
subject: Spreadsheet
explanation: Excel uses A1 and R1C1 referencing styles (2 styles).

question: To insert Auto sum function, one can press..........
subject: Spreadsheet
explanation: Alt+= inserts the AutoSum function.

question: The category axis is also called a..........
subject: Spreadsheet
explanation: Category axis is also known as X-Axis.

question: The shortcut key is used to create default chart is..........
subject: Spreadsheet
explanation: Both F11 and Alt+F1 create default charts in Excel.

question: The file extension of workspace file is..........
subject: Spreadsheet
explanation: Excel workspace file extension is .xlw.

question: The shortcut key is used to switch contents in to the edit mode..........
subject: Spreadsheet
explanation: F2 switches cell to edit mode.

question: Which one of Excel window allows you to enter values in worksheet?
subject: Spreadsheet
explanation: Formula Bar allows entering values directly.

question: A typical worksheet has number of columns.
subject: Spreadsheet
explanation: Excel worksheet has 256 columns.

question: Comments put in cells are called..........
subject: Spreadsheet
explanation: Cell comments appear as Cell Tips on hover.

question: Getting data from a cell located in a different sheet is called..........
subject: Spreadsheet
explanation: Getting data from another sheet is called referencing.

question: Which of the following is not a valid data type in Excel?
subject: Spreadsheet
explanation: Hyperlink is not a data type; it is an object/feature.

question: Which elements of a worksheet can be protected from accidental modification?
subject: Spreadsheet
explanation: Contents, objects, and scenarios can all be protected.

question: A numeric value can be treated as a label value if it is preceded with..........
subject: Spreadsheet
explanation: Preceding a number with apostrophe treats it as text/label.

question: Which is text operator?
subject: Spreadsheet
explanation: Ampersand (&) is the text concatenation operator in Excel.

question: Which formula returns multiple results from single formula?
subject: Spreadsheet
explanation: Array formulas can return multiple results from a single formula.

question: Which is not tool for what-if analysis?
subject: Spreadsheet
explanation: Pivot table is for data summarization, not what-if analysis.

question: Data can be added from different sheet within a workbook using..........
subject: Spreadsheet
explanation: Data Consolidate combines data from multiple sheets.

question: The Edit > Delete command will delete..........
subject: Spreadsheet
explanation: Edit > Delete can delete sheet, contents, or cells.

question: Data of sheet can be printed on center of page using..........
subject: Spreadsheet
explanation: Page Setup > Margin settings center content on page.

question: To import data for database in excel using..........
subject: Spreadsheet
explanation: Data > Import External Data is used to import database data.

question: The mode of operations can be display at..........
subject: Spreadsheet
explanation: Status bar displays the current mode of operation.

question: AutoSum button appears at the toolbar.
subject: Spreadsheet
explanation: AutoSum button appears on the Standard toolbar.

question: You can enable auto save feature using..........
subject: Spreadsheet
explanation: AutoSave settings are found in Options.

question: The insert function appears at the..........
subject: Spreadsheet
explanation: Insert Function appears in the Formula palette.

question: Which command will display the subset of data from list?
subject: Spreadsheet
explanation: Filter command displays a subset of data from a list.

question: Which one you can search by find command?
subject: Spreadsheet
explanation: Find command can search formulas, values, and comments.

question: Which will affects the column width of entire worksheet?
subject: Spreadsheet
explanation: Standard width affects the default column width of entire worksheet.

question: Which symbol will display when your number is too larger than column width?
subject: Spreadsheet
explanation: # symbols appear when a number is too wide for the column.
